name: http
label: HTTP
description: Versatile generic HTTP connector. Supports any REST API: configurable auth (None / Basic / Bearer / API Key Header / API Key Query Param / OAuth2 Client Credentials with token caching), per-call auth override, connection-level custom headers, query params, body shaping (json / form / multipart / raw), retry with backoff, follow-redirects toggle, response path plucking, RFC 5988 / next-URL / page-param pagination, and scheduled data ingestion.

ingestion_config_schema.title: Fetch URL
ingestion_config_schema.name: fetch_url
ingestion_config_schema.tooltip: Absolute URL or path (relative to the configured Server URL) to call on each scheduled ingestion run.

ingestion_config_schema.title: Response Path
ingestion_config_schema.name: response_path
ingestion_config_schema.tooltip: Dot/bracket path to the records list inside the response body (e.g. 'data.items', 'results[0].records'). Leave blank if the body itself is a list.

ingestion_config_schema.title: Pagination Mode
ingestion_config_schema.name: pagination_mode
ingestion_config_schema.tooltip: How to walk paginated responses. link_header = RFC 5988 Link header. next_url_path = next URL is at a JSON path. page_param = bump a numeric query parameter.

fields.title: Server URL
fields.name: server_url
fields.tooltip: Base URL prepended to relative request paths. Leave blank if every action will use absolute URLs.
fields.description: Base URL prepended to relative request paths. Leave blank if every action will use absolute URLs.

fields.title: Port
fields.name: port
fields.tooltip: Optional port appended to the Server URL when the URL does not already specify one.

fields.title: Authentication Type
fields.name: auth_type
fields.tooltip: Default authentication method applied to every request. Individual actions can override this per-call.
fields.description: Default authentication method. Per-action override is supported.

Token Login.title: Login URL
Token Login.name: login_url
Token Login.tooltip: Endpoint to POST credentials to. Relative paths join the Server URL.

Token Login.title: Login Body Type
Token Login.name: login_body_type
Token Login.tooltip: json: send username/password as a JSON body. header_only: send no body — credentials go in Login Request Headers only (e.g. Yeti x-yeti-apikey).

Token Login.title: Login Request Headers
Token Login.name: login_request_headers
Token Login.tooltip: JSON object of headers to send on the login request. Required for header_only body type. Also accepted for json mode (merged with the credential body).

Token Login.title: Username
Token Login.name: login_username
Token Login.tooltip: Required for json body type. Leave blank when using header_only.

Token Login.title: Password
Token Login.name: login_password
Token Login.tooltip: Required for json body type. Leave blank when using header_only.

Token Login.title: Username Field
Token Login.name: login_username_field
Token Login.tooltip: JSON body key for the username. Default 'username'.

Token Login.title: Token Response Path
Token Login.name: login_token_path
Token Login.tooltip: Dot-path inside the JSON login response. Leave blank if the response body is the bare token string (e.g. filebrowser).

Token Login.title: Auth Header Name
Token Login.name: login_header_name
Token Login.tooltip: Header to send the token on. e.g. 'X-Auth' (filebrowser), 'Authorization' (with Bearer prefix).

Token Login.title: Auth Header Prefix
Token Login.name: login_header_prefix
Token Login.tooltip: Optional prefix prepended to the token. e.g. 'Bearer ' (note trailing space).

fields.title: Default Custom Headers
fields.name: default_headers
fields.tooltip: Non-sensitive headers attached to every request (e.g. {"User-Agent":"FortiSOAR"}). DO NOT put secrets here — values are stored in plaintext. For secret headers use 'API Key Header' auth.
fields.description: Non-sensitive headers attached to every request. Values are stored in plaintext.

fields.title: Max Retries
fields.name: max_retries
fields.tooltip: Number of automatic retries on transient failures. 0 disables retry.

fields.title: Retry Backoff Factor
fields.name: backoff_factor
fields.tooltip: urllib3 backoff factor. Sleep between retries = factor * (2^(retry-1)).

fields.title: Retry On Status
fields.name: retry_on_status
fields.tooltip: Comma-separated HTTP status codes to retry.

fields.title: Return On HTTP Error
fields.name: return_on_error
fields.tooltip: When true, non-2xx responses are returned to the playbook (status_code + body) instead of raising.

operations.title: HTTP Request (Any Method)
operations.description: Perform an HTTP request with a user-selected method. Most flexible action.

parameters.title: Method
parameters.name: method
parameters.tooltip: HTTP verb to use for this request.

parameters.title: URL or Path
parameters.name: rest_api
parameters.tooltip: Absolute URL or path relative to the configured Server URL. e.g. /api/v1/things or https://other.example.com/endpoint

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: parameter
parameters.tooltip: Key/value pairs appended to the URL as query string. Accepts {"key":"value"} or [{"key":"k","value":"v"}] format.

parameters.title: Headers
parameters.name: header
parameters.tooltip: Additional HTTP headers for this request. Merged with Default Custom Headers from the connection config; per-call values win on conflict.

parameters.title: Body Type
parameters.name: body_type
parameters.tooltip: How to encode the request body. json = JSON-encoded object. form = URL-encoded form fields. raw = send the string as-is. multipart = multipart/form-data (file upload). none = no body.

parameters.title: Body
parameters.name: data
parameters.tooltip: Body payload. Shape depends on Body Type. For 'raw' a string is accepted. For 'multipart' use a dict of fields.

parameters.title: Timeout (seconds, override)
parameters.name: timeout
parameters.tooltip: Per-request timeout in seconds. Overrides the connection-level timeout for this step only.

parameters.title: Follow Redirects
parameters.name: follow_redirects
parameters.tooltip: When enabled, HTTP 3xx redirects are followed automatically. Disable to inspect the redirect response directly.

parameters.title: Response Path
parameters.name: response_path
parameters.tooltip: Dot/bracket path to pluck from the JSON response body before returning. e.g. 'data.results' or 'items[0].id'. Leave blank to return the full body.

parameters.title: Raw Response (binary-safe)
parameters.name: raw_response
parameters.tooltip: Return content as base64 instead of parsing. Use for binary downloads.

parameters.name: rest_api

parameters.name: raw_response
parameters.tooltip: Return raw bytes as base64 instead of parsing the body. Use for binary downloads, PDFs, or responses with non-JSON content types.

operations.title: HTTP Paginate
operations.description: Walk a paginated endpoint and concatenate the results. Supports RFC 5988 Link header, next-URL-in-body, and bumped page-param modes.

parameters.title: URL or Path
parameters.name: rest_api
parameters.tooltip: Absolute URL or path relative to the configured Server URL. e.g. /api/v1/things or https://other.example.com/endpoint

parameters.title: Method
parameters.name: method
parameters.tooltip: HTTP verb to use for this request.

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: parameter
parameters.tooltip: Key/value pairs appended to the URL as query string. Accepts {"key":"value"} or [{"key":"k","value":"v"}] format.

parameters.title: Headers
parameters.name: header
parameters.tooltip: Additional HTTP headers for this request. Merged with Default Custom Headers from the connection config; per-call values win on conflict.

parameters.title: Body Type
parameters.name: body_type
parameters.tooltip: How to encode the request body. json = JSON-encoded object. form = URL-encoded form fields. raw = send the string as-is. multipart = multipart/form-data (file upload). none = no body.

parameters.title: Body
parameters.name: data
parameters.tooltip: Request body payload. Shape depends on Body Type. For json/form pass a dict; for raw pass a string; for multipart pass a dict of field names to values.

parameters.title: Pagination Mode
parameters.name: pagination_mode
parameters.tooltip: How to advance through pages. link_header = follow RFC 5988 Link: <url>; rel=next header. next_url_path = next URL is at a dot-path inside the JSON body. page_param = increment a numeric query parameter.

parameters.title: Items Path
parameters.name: items_path
parameters.tooltip: Dot/bracket path to the list of records inside each page's response body. e.g. 'data.results'. Leave blank if the body itself is the list.

parameters.title: Next URL Path (next_url_path mode)
parameters.name: next_url_path
parameters.tooltip: Dot/bracket path inside the response body where the next-page URL is found. Only used in next_url_path mode. e.g. 'links.next' or 'meta.nextUrl'.

parameters.title: Page Param Name (page_param mode)
parameters.name: page_param_name
parameters.tooltip: Query parameter name to increment for each page. Only used in page_param mode. e.g. 'page' or 'offset'.

parameters.title: Start Page
parameters.name: start_page
parameters.tooltip: Starting page number for page_param mode.

parameters.title: Max Pages
parameters.name: max_pages
parameters.tooltip: Maximum number of pages to fetch before stopping, regardless of whether more pages exist. Guards against runaway pagination.

operations.title: Fetch Records (Ingestion)
operations.description: Calls a configured endpoint, optionally paginates, plucks records via response_path, and returns them as a flat list. Used by scheduled data ingestion and available on-demand.

parameters.title: Fetch URL
parameters.name: fetch_url
parameters.tooltip: Absolute URL or path to call for each scheduled ingestion run. Overrides the connection-level Fetch URL if set.

parameters.title: Method
parameters.name: method
parameters.tooltip: HTTP verb to use for this request.

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: parameter
parameters.tooltip: Key/value pairs appended to the URL as query string. Accepts {"key":"value"} or [{"key":"k","value":"v"}] format.

parameters.title: Headers
parameters.name: header
parameters.tooltip: Additional HTTP headers for this request. Merged with Default Custom Headers from the connection config; per-call values win on conflict.

parameters.title: Body Type
parameters.name: body_type
parameters.tooltip: How to encode the request body. json = JSON-encoded object. form = URL-encoded form fields. raw = send the string as-is. multipart = multipart/form-data (file upload). none = no body.

parameters.title: Body
parameters.name: data
parameters.tooltip: Request body payload. Shape depends on Body Type. For json/form pass a dict; for raw pass a string; for multipart pass a dict of field names to values.

parameters.title: Response Path
parameters.name: response_path
parameters.tooltip: Dot/bracket path to pluck from the JSON response body before returning. e.g. 'data.results' or 'items[0].id'. Leave blank to return the full body.

parameters.title: Pagination Mode
parameters.name: pagination_mode
parameters.tooltip: How to advance through pages. link_header = follow RFC 5988 Link: <url>; rel=next header. next_url_path = next URL is at a dot-path inside the JSON body. page_param = increment a numeric query parameter.

parameters.title: Next URL Path (next_url_path mode)
parameters.name: next_url_path
parameters.tooltip: Dot/bracket path inside the response body where the next-page URL is found. Only used in next_url_path mode. e.g. 'links.next' or 'meta.nextUrl'.

parameters.title: Page Param Name (page_param mode)
parameters.name: page_param_name
parameters.tooltip: Query parameter name to increment for each page. Only used in page_param mode. e.g. 'page' or 'offset'.

parameters.title: Max Pages
parameters.name: max_pages
parameters.tooltip: Maximum number of pages to fetch before stopping, regardless of whether more pages exist. Guards against runaway pagination.

operations.title: Upload File
operations.description: Stream-upload a file to an arbitrary HTTP endpoint. Accepts a FortiSOAR attachment @id, file IRI, or attachment record. Supports multipart and raw-body modes; never buffers the file through the playbook engine.

parameters.title: URL or Path
parameters.name: rest_api
parameters.tooltip: Destination. {filename} placeholder is substituted with the source file's name (URL-encoded).

parameters.title: Method
parameters.name: method
parameters.tooltip: HTTP verb to use for this request.

parameters.title: Attachment @id / IRI
parameters.name: attachment_id
parameters.tooltip: Pass the attachment's @id, file IRI, or an attachment record object.

parameters.title: File IRI (alternative)
parameters.name: file_iri
parameters.tooltip: FortiSOAR File IRI (e.g. /api/3/files/<uuid>). Alternative to Attachment @id — use when you already have a direct file reference.

parameters.title: Local File Path (advanced)
parameters.name: file_path
parameters.tooltip: Optional. Absolute path on the integration agent; bypasses attachment lookup.

parameters.title: Upload Mode
parameters.name: upload_mode
parameters.tooltip: multipart = standard form-file upload. raw_body = file bytes are the request body (filebrowser, S3 PUT, etc.).

parameters.title: Multipart Field Name
parameters.name: field_name
parameters.tooltip: Multipart form field name for the file part. Default is 'file'. Only applies in multipart mode.

parameters.title: Extra Multipart Fields
parameters.name: extra_fields
parameters.tooltip: Additional form fields to send alongside the file (multipart mode only).

parameters.title: Content Type
parameters.name: content_type
parameters.tooltip: MIME type sent as the file's Content-Type. e.g. 'text/csv', 'application/pdf'. Defaults to application/octet-stream.

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: parameter
parameters.tooltip: Key/value pairs appended to the URL as query string. Accepts {"key":"value"} or [{"key":"k","value":"v"}] format.

parameters.title: Headers
parameters.name: header
parameters.tooltip: Additional HTTP headers for this request. Merged with Default Custom Headers from the connection config; per-call values win on conflict.

parameters.title: Timeout (seconds, override)
parameters.name: timeout
parameters.tooltip: Per-request timeout in seconds. Overrides the connection-level timeout for this step only.

parameters.title: Follow Redirects
parameters.name: follow_redirects
parameters.tooltip: When enabled, HTTP 3xx redirects are followed automatically. Disable to inspect the redirect response directly.

parameters.title: Response Path
parameters.name: response_path
parameters.tooltip: Dot/bracket path to pluck from the JSON response body before returning. e.g. 'data.results' or 'items[0].id'. Leave blank to return the full body.

parameters.title: Raw Response (binary-safe)
parameters.name: raw_response
parameters.tooltip: Return raw bytes as base64 instead of parsing the body. Use for binary downloads, PDFs, or responses with non-JSON content types.

operations.title: Download File
operations.description: Download a remote URL and save the response body as a FortiSOAR attachment (or to disk on the integration agent). Streams bytes — large files never go through the playbook engine. The reverse of Upload File.

parameters.title: URL or Path
parameters.name: rest_api
parameters.tooltip: Absolute URL or path relative to the configured Server URL. e.g. /api/v1/things or https://other.example.com/endpoint

parameters.title: Method
parameters.name: method
parameters.tooltip: GET for normal downloads. POST only if the endpoint requires a request body to produce the file.

parameters.title: Create FortiSOAR Attachment
parameters.name: create_attachment
parameters.tooltip: When true (default), creates an Attachment record visible in File Management. When false, leaves the file on the integration agent and returns its path.

parameters.title: Attachment Name
parameters.name: attachment_name
parameters.tooltip: Display name for the Attachment record. Defaults to the resolved filename.

parameters.title: Description
parameters.name: description
parameters.tooltip: Description stored on the Attachment record in FortiSOAR.

parameters.title: Filename Override
parameters.name: filename
parameters.tooltip: Force a specific filename. Otherwise the connector uses Content-Disposition, then the URL basename, then 'download.bin'.

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: parameter
parameters.tooltip: Key/value pairs appended to the URL as query string. Accepts {"key":"value"} or [{"key":"k","value":"v"}] format.

parameters.title: Headers
parameters.name: header
parameters.tooltip: Additional HTTP headers for this request. Merged with Default Custom Headers from the connection config; per-call values win on conflict.

parameters.title: Body Type
parameters.name: body_type
parameters.tooltip: How to encode the request body. json = JSON-encoded object. form = URL-encoded form fields. raw = send the string as-is. multipart = multipart/form-data (file upload). none = no body.

parameters.title: Body
parameters.name: data
parameters.tooltip: Request body payload. Shape depends on Body Type. For json/form pass a dict; for raw pass a string; for multipart pass a dict of field names to values.

parameters.title: Timeout (seconds, override)
parameters.name: timeout
parameters.tooltip: Per-request timeout in seconds. Overrides the connection-level timeout for this step only.

parameters.title: Follow Redirects
parameters.name: follow_redirects
parameters.tooltip: When enabled, HTTP 3xx redirects are followed automatically. Disable to inspect the redirect response directly.
